---
title: "R codes, packages, examples - Reminder for myself"
author: "Kadir Jun Ayhan"
date: last-modified
draft: true
categories: [useful links, R]
---

I have used emails, bookmarks, reminders, notes, docx etc. for good practices or links I have seen. But they are very unorganized as it stands. This post which I will keep updating is to keep this list more organized and to remind myself about them.

# Quarto

## Website - blog

Very good website, blog examples:

-   [Andrew Heiss](https://www.andrewheiss.com/)

-   [Mine Dogucu](https://www.minedogucu.com/)

-   [Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel](https://mine-cr.com/)

-   [Dr. Léo Belzile's class](https://edsm.rbind.io/)

Embedding social media posts: an extension: [quarto-social-embeds](https://github.com/sellorm/quarto-social-embeds)

# Bayesian Data Analysis

Bayestest: [ROPE](https://easystats.github.io/bayestestR/articles/region_of_practical_equivalence.html) Bayestest: [HDI](https://easystats.github.io/bayestestR/reference/hdi.html) Bayestest: [Guidelines](https://easystats.github.io/bayestestR/articles/guidelines.html) [Marginal effects](https://vincentarelbundock.github.io/marginaleffects/articles/brms.html) [A guide to correctly calculating posterior predictions and average marginal effects with multilievel Bayesian models - Andrew Heiss](https://www.andrewheiss.com/blog/2021/11/10/ame-bayes-re-guide/) [brmsmargins: A package that has functions to calculate marginal effects from brms models](https://joshuawiley.com/brmsmargins/)
